claim: Letta Evals framework enables testing stateful agents by replicating agent state from snapshots (agent files) and running them against evaluation datasets
actionable: For evaluating stateful agents, create agent file snapshots and replicate one agent per test sample; this enables parallel evaluation and git-versioning of agent configurations.
source_title: How to Evaluate Stateful Agents: Starter Guide to Letta Evals

claim: Tool-based grading (e.g., contains ground truth) works for factual Q&A; rubric-based grading with LLM-as-judge needed for qualitative evaluation
actionable: Use contains/exact-match grading for factual tasks; use rubric-based LLM grading for open-ended tasks like writing quality or explanation completeness.
source_title: How to Evaluate Stateful Agents: Starter Guide to Letta Evals

claim: Multi-turn evaluation dataset format (list of messages per observation) enables testing agent memory retention across conversation turns
actionable: Structure multi-turn eval datasets with message lists; extract memory blocks to verify agents retain information from earlier turns.
source_title: How to Evaluate Stateful Agents: Starter Guide to Letta Evals

claim: Gates (pass/fail thresholds) enable integration of agent evals into CI/CD pipelines to prevent performance regressions on code changes
actionable: Define gates (e.g., accuracy >= 70%) to fail builds when agent changes cause performance regression; integrate evals into GitHub Actions or CI pipeline.
source_title: How to Evaluate Stateful Agents: Starter Guide to Letta Evals

claim: Agent files (JSON snapshots) act as 'agents as code' and can be version-controlled in git repos, enabling reproducible evaluations across commits
actionable: Commit agent files to git; this enables testing if agent behavior regressed after prompt/config changes in pull requests.
source_title: How to Evaluate Stateful Agents: Starter Guide to Letta Evals

claim: Stateful agents can improve over time through repeated evaluation-feedback cycles (custom teacher agents improve by evaluating many samples)
actionable: Design long-lived agents that learn from repeated evaluations; create specialized judge agents that accumulate expertise from evaluating many examples.
source_title: How to Evaluate Stateful Agents: Starter Guide to Letta Evals
